content:
CRITICAL FIRST STEP:
1. Read the LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT included in this prompt. Treat it as the authoritative assignment for this round.
2. If you need the stored context verbatim or want to inspect changes, use context_read(action="read", name="Delegation Instructions") before proceeding.
3. Do NOT proceed based on memory from earlier rounds when the live snapshot is present.

GENERATION MODE:
IMPORTANT: The ontology has exactly ONE namespace: <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>. Type every new instance's rdf:type with an IRI in this EXACT namespace (e.g. <https://example.org/ns/riepr#YourClass>, or declare `@prefix : <https://example.org/ns/riepr#>` yourself and use `:YourClass`). Do NOT bind `:`/`ex:` or any other prefix to a different namespace, and do NOT invent any other namespace either - not a generic placeholder, not a variant of this one, not a per-topic or per-class namespace of your own. Every class lives in the ONE namespace above, with no exceptions.
RICHNESS PROFILE: RICH
- Generate required SHACL triples and substantial realistic optional enrichment (context, relationships, provenance, temporal details).
- You may invent new predicates for useful domain facts when existing ontology predicates are insufficient. Keep them consistent and reusable.

- If NO instances of your assigned target class exist, CREATE them from scratch.
- Generate realistic instances that satisfy SHACL constraints.
- Type every new instance with the EXACT target class URI from the SHACL/Ontology context (the
  same namespace as the shapes' sh:targetClass). Do NOT invent a different or placeholder
  namespace, even one that looks plausible or related — an instance typed in the wrong namespace
  will not match any shape, so it will never actually be validated.
- A shape's own name is the name of a validation rule, not necessarily a class - never assume it
  is one. Always look up your assigned shape's sh:targetClass property in the SHACL context and
  use THAT value's exact URI as the rdf:type for new instances.
- Reuse existing ontology/vocabulary predicates; do not invent new ones (see delegated shape's
  sh:path values and the ontology context for what already exists).
- Look up your assigned target class's `hydra:search [ a hydra:IriTemplate ; hydra:template "..." ;
  hydra:mapping [...] ]` in the Ontology context and mint new instance IRIs by substituting real
  values into that exact template. Only fall back to another URI pattern/convention if the class
  has no hydra:search template. A resolved template contains `/` in its path, so write it as a
  full IRI in angle brackets (`<...>`) - NEVER as a `prefix:LocalName` prefixed name, since a
  prefixed name's local part cannot contain an unescaped `/` and will fail to parse.
- Create required supporting resources when needed.
- Mint a named IRI for every instance you create. Do NOT use blank nodes (`[ ... ]` or `_:id`) for primary domain entities; reserve them for genuinely anonymous auxiliary structures only (e.g. RDF list cells).
- Do NOT include `#` comments in the Turtle you write (no worker/pool/range bookkeeping notes) — only RDF triples.

QUALITY-FIRST RULES:
- Work ONLY on your assigned target class from delegation context.
- Make minimum changes required for conformance.
- Do not modify other workers' target classes.
- Do NOT edit unrelated resources just to reduce global violation count.

VALIDATION LOOP:
- After changes, run shacl_validator(source="store").
- Fix only violations tied to your assigned shape(s)/focus nodes.
- If unrelated violations remain, report and stop.
- If there are no violations for your assigned shape/focus nodes in this pass, do not write triples and report VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE.
- If shacl_validator reports unrecognized predicates/classes on subjects you just created, remove
  those specific triples with triplestore_remove and re-add them using only predicates/classes
  from the delegated shape's sh:path values or the ontology context. Do not report BLOCKED for
  vocabulary you invented yourself - fix it and re-validate instead.

CHANGE SAFETY:
- Prefer additive fixes over destructive removals.
- Remove triples only when required to resolve a specific violation.
- Keep URI/prefix usage consistent with existing data.



DONE CRITERIA:
- At least one valid instance of your assigned target class exists (or more if cardinality requires).
- Assigned shape(s) have no remaining violations.
- Required properties/cardinalities are satisfied.

MANDATORY REPORTING (TOOL, NOT FREE TEXT):
- You MUST call:
  worker_progress(
    status=<CREATED|FIXED|VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE|BLOCKED>,
    target_shape=<shape name>,
    target_class=<class>,
    changed_triples_count=<integer>,
    created_or_updated_subjects=<comma-separated URIs or NONE>,
    validation_result=<CONFORMS|NON_CONFORMS>,
    remaining_issues=<short text or NONE>
  )
- Then provide a brief human summary (1-3 lines) consistent with the tool payload.


Assignment context: shapes 6-7 (worker 4 of 4). Follow your delegated task exactly (generate or fix as instructed). Use triplestore_add/remove/read and validation tools as needed.

LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT (authoritative for this round; use this even if you do not call context_read):
TASK: Process FilterShape (class :Filter) and OperationLocationShape (class :OperationLocation). CREATE new instances for both classes.

IRI PATTERNS:
- Filter (NO hydra:search - use default prefix): <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter-{uuid}> a :Filter ; ...
- OperationLocation (from hydra:search): <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/{geoId}/{issued}/{created}> a :OperationLocation ; ...

SUBSTITUTE real values for {uuid} or {geoId}, {issued} (xsd:date), {created} (xsd:dateTime). Use xsd:dateTime format "2024-01-15T10:00:00" for created and xsd:date format "2024-01-15" for issued.

MANDATORY SHACL CONSTRAINTS (minCount=1):
- Filter: rdfs:label, dc:issued, dc:created, dc:modified, adms:status, sosa:isHostedBy (:OperationLocation)
  Optional: ogc:hasGeometry, dc:type, dc:valid, ssn:hasSubSystem, prov:wasRevisionOf, adms:identifier, ssn:hasDeployment
- OperationLocation: rdfs:label, dc:issued, dc:created, dc:modified, prov:wasAttributedTo (:Operator), ogc:hasGeometry (exactly 1)
  Optional: prov:hadPrimarySource (:org:Site), locn:address, prov:wasRevisionOf, adms:identifier, prov:wasInfluencedBy, ssn:inDeployment

EXAMPLE Filter:
<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter-flt-001> a :Filter ;
  rdfs:label "Particulate Matter Filter PMF-001"@en ;
  dc:issued "2024-01-15"^^xsd:date ;
  dc:created "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  dc:modified "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  adms:status :actief ;
  sosa:isHostedBy <https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> .

EXAMPLE OperationLocation (must have exactly 1 geometry):
<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> a :OperationLocation ;
  rdfs:label "Glass Factory Antwerp"@en ;
  dc:issued "2024-01-15"^^xsd:date ;
  dc:created "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  dc:modified "2024-01-15T10:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
  prov:wasAttributedTo <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-op-001> ;
  ogc:hasGeometry [ rdf:type ogc:Geometry ; ogc:asWKT "POINT(4.4025 51.2194)"^^ogc:wktLiteral ] .

PREDICATES MUST come from ontology/SHACL context only - never invent predicates. Each triplestore_add is validated as standalone Turtle.

REPORT: worker_progress(status, target_shape, target_class, changed_triples_count, validation_result, remaining_issues)

If this live snapshot differs from your memory of earlier rounds, follow this snapshot. Use context_read to inspect the stored context verbatim when needed.
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","name":"Delegation Instructions","length":50000.0})
triplestore_read({"mode":"count"})
{"message":"The shared triple store contains 0 triples.","count":0,"status":"success"}

Good. The store is empty. My task is to CREATE new instances for both :Filter and :OperationLocation classes. I need to:

1. Create at least one :Filter instance with all mandatory properties
2. Create at least one :OperationLocation instance with all mandatory properties (including exactly 1 ogc:hasGeometry)
3. Ensure the Filter references the OperationLocation via sosa:isHostedBy

Let me create a realistic set of data following the patterns specified:

For Filter, I'll use UUID-style identifiers like "Filter-filt-001"
For OperationLocation, I need to follow the hydra template pattern with geoId/issued/created

I'll create one Filter and one OperationLocation that are linked together.
{"total_triples":7,"triples_added":7,"message":"Added 7 triples. Shared store now contains 7 triples.","status":"success"}
{"total_triples":18,"triples_added":11,"message":"Added 11 triples. Shared store now contains 18 triples.","status":"success"}
Now let me validate the store to ensure conformance:
shacl_validator({"source":"store","format-on-error":true})

The validation shows several issues:

1. **Min violations** - properties that I set like adms:status are showing "minCount[1]: Invalid cardinality". This seems contradictory since the delegated task says they are MANDATORY constraints, not OPTIONAL. The issue might be with how SHACL is configured - perhaps these predicates have a shape declaring minCount=1 but my data doesn't match.

Wait - the error messages show that for Filter-filt-001:
- adms:status has "Got count = 0" even though I set it to :actief
- Same issue with dc:modified which I set a value for

And for OperationLocation, similar issues exist. There's clearly something wrong here - the validation says I got 0 values but I did provide them.

Looking more carefully at the warnings: "6 predicate(s) are not part of the ontology/SHACL vocabulary (invented rather than reused)" - it's flagging dc:created, dc:modified, etc as invented predicates! This is strange because they're standard DC properties. The validator seems confused about the namespace.

Let me check what triples actually exist in the store now and also examine if there are existing shapes that define constraints:
triplestore_read({"mode":"all"})

The data is present. The issue seems to be that the validator doesn't recognize dc: as having values - which could be because it's using dcterms (http://purl.org/dc/terms/) vs dc (http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/). Let me check if I need to use dct prefixes instead, or if there are namespace conflicts in the validator itself.

Looking at the error messages again - it shows `dc: created / issued / modified` from `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/` but perhaps the SHACL shapes reference these properties using different URIs (like dc:title vs dct:issued?). The delegated task mentions `dc:issued`, `dc:created`, and `dc:modified` which I used correctly according to the delegation instructions.

However, the validator is rejecting them as if they have 0 values, when clearly they do exist in the store. This suggests a problem with either:
1. The triples not being properly indexed in the validator context
2. A mismatch between how DC predicates are defined vs expected

Looking at the vocabulary warning more carefully - it says these predcates are "invented" meaning they're not part of the ontology/SHACL vocabulary. But I was told to reuse existing predicates, so maybe I need to verify what's actually in the ontology context...

Let me look for any stored delegation or schema context to understand what predicates are recognized:
context_reader({"action":"list"})

{"changed_count":5,"contexts":[{"changed":true,"name":"example file","type":"text/plain","size":613},{"changed":true,"name":"Ontology","type":"text/turtle","size":100053},{"changed":true,"name":"Validation Report","type":"text/plain","size":7626},{"changed":true,"name":"Ontology Summary","type":"text/plain","size":1465},{"changed":true,"name":"Worker Progress Report","type":"text/plain","size":0},{"changed":false,"name":"Delegation Instructions","type":"text/plain","size":2308}],"message":"There are 5 changed context(s) since your last query","count":6}
Good, the contexts have changed. Let me check the Ontology context to understand what predicates/classes are recognized in the SHACL shapes:
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":0.0})

Key discovery! The Ontology defines the `dc` prefix as `http://purl.org/dc/terms/`, NOT `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/`. I used the wrong namespace for dc predicates. Let me look at the Filter class definition that was shown:

From the ontology, :Filter class constraints include (minCardinality 1):
- `rdfs:label`
- `dc:created` 
- `dc:modified`
- `dc:issued`
- `adms:status`
- `sosa:isHostedBy`

So I needed to use `http://purl.org/dc/terms/` not `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/`. Let me fix this by removing the wrongly typed Filter and OperationLocation instances, then re-adding them with the correct prefix.

First let me check if there are existing Operator or Site classes I need to understand:
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":30000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":30000.0})
I can see the Operator class. Now let me check for OperationLocation in the ontology:
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":40000.0,"name":"Ontology","start":60000.0})

I now have a clear picture:

For **OperationLocation**, I need exactly 1 `ogc:hasGeometry` which contains an `ogc:Geometry` class with `ogc:asWKT`. But importantly, the ontology shows that the `dc` prefix is bound to `http://purl.org/dc/terms/`, not `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/`.

The key issue from validation was that properties like `adms:status`, `dc:created`, etc are showing count=0, which seems wrong since I set them. This might be a validator issue, but let me re-add the data with proper prefixes and make sure it matches exactly what's expected in the ontology.

Looking at OperationLocation constraints:
- Required (minCardinality 1): `dc:created`, `dc:modified`, `dc:issued`, `ogc:hasGeometry` (exactly 1), `prov:wasAttributedTo` (:Operator), `rdfs:label`
- Optional: others

Note that the example in the delegation has a blank node for geometry. Let me clear my previous data and re-add correctly. But since dc prefix is http://purl.org/dc/terms/, I should keep using that but maybe the validator had issues with how it's parsing things.

Let me first remove what I added:
triplestore_remove({"subject":"https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter-filt-001"})
{"triples_removed":7,"total_triples":95,"message":"Removed 7 triples (including any orphaned blank nodes). Shared store now contains 95 triples.","status":"success"}
triplestore_remove({"subject":"https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00"})
{"triples_removed":9,"total_triples":86,"message":"Removed 9 triples (including any orphaned blank nodes). Shared store now contains 86 triples.","status":"success"}
Now let me check what's remaining in the triplestore to understand if there were existing entities:
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","predicate":"rdf:type"})

{"triples":["<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Glass-Corp> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/locn#Address> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#Geometry> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/locn#Address> .","<https://example.org/id/meetpunt/meet-uuid-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementPoint> .","<https://example.org/id/onttrekkingspunt/ont-uuid-002/2024-01-16/2024-01-16T11:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#WithdrawalPoint> .","<null> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#Geometry> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Filter> .","<https://example.org/id/meetpunt/meet-uuid-002/2024-01-16/2024-01-16T11:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementPoint> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Cement-NV> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> .","<https://example.org/id/onttrekkingspunt/ont-uuid-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#WithdrawalPoint> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-cement-mechelen/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation> .","<https://example.org/id/proces/procs-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Process> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument-inst-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#MeasurementInstrument> .","<https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-op-001> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator> ."],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 16 triples from the shared store.","count":16}

Good - there ARE existing OperationLocation instances already in the store:
- `https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-cement-mechelen/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00`
- `https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00`

Also there's an existing :Operator (`https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-op-001`) that I can use. 

The task says CREATE new instances - so I should create fresh ones, not fix/reuse existing. Let me now properly add a new Filter and OperationLocation. The dc prefix in the ontology is `http://purl.org/dc/terms/` which is what I'll use.

Let me create one :Filter instance and make sure it references an :OperationLocation. For simplicity, I'll point to the existing geo-glass-antwerp location or create a new one linked properly. Let me check what properties are on those existing OperationLocations:
triplestore_read({"mode":"query","subject":"https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00"})

{"triples":["<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label> \"Glass Factory Antwerp\" .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/created> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\" .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/issued> \"2024-01-15\" .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/modified> \"2024-01-15T10:00:00\" .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#hasGeometry> <null> .","<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-glass-antwerp/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00> <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#wasAttributedTo> <https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Glass-Corp> ."],"status":"success","message":"Query returned 7 triples from the shared store.","count":7}

I can see this existing OperationLocation has:
- `prov:wasAttributedTo` pointing to `https://example.org/ns/riepr#Operator-Glass-Corp` 
- It's missing dc:type label (should maybe have @en)
- The geometry is a blank node

But since my task is to CREATE new instances, and the validation showed issues with predicates even when values exist, let me create a simple complete example. I'll need to use fully qualified URIs or correct prefix declarations.

Looking at the ontology header: `@prefix dc: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>` - so I need that exact declaration. Let me create new data properly:
{"total_triples":91,"triples_added":11,"message":"Added 11 triples. Shared store now contains 91 triples.","status":"success"}

Wait - I created the Filter referencing an existing OperationLocation which is fine, but I need to make sure ALL mandatory properties for both classes are satisfied. The validation errors suggested the values weren't being recognized properly. Let me check what happened and validate:
shacl_validator({"source":"store","format-on-error":true})

Interesting! The key new violation I can see now:

1. `ClassConstraint[<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>]: Expected class :<https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>` for value `:actief`

This shows that the validator thinks `adms:status` must point to something in the SHACL vocabulary as a Status class (defined as `<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>`), but I'm using my own prefixed `:actief`. Let me check - in the ontology, adms is declared as `@prefix adms: <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#>`, and I saw that `:actief` is defined as a skos:Concept AND adms:Status. But somehow it's not being recognized correctly by the validator.

Looking more carefully, the error says "Expected class `<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>`" - this might mean the SHACL shapes are checking against http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/ or some other vocabulary. But wait, I think the issue is different - it's using a fulliri rather than my prefix expansion. 

Actually looking at the validation report more carefully: "Expected class :<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>" - this seems to show that SHACL expects instances of adms:Status where `:` refers to something, but the status concept I'm using should work...

Let me check if there are existing :actief and :inactief statuses in my triplestore:
triplestore_read({"predicate":"rdfs:type"})

I see there's a `locn:Address` instance in my triplestore which I added, and it references an unqualified class `ogc:Geometry`. The error message mentions these as not recognized. There are many existing instances that use `:actief` as their status value successfully (`adms:status :actief`).

The core issue seems to be related specifically to how validation is checking the OperationLocation and Filter constraints. Looking at the validation report more carefully, the errors for my newly created instances show different things:

For `Filter-new-001`:
- The adms:status error says "Expected class :<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status> for <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>" - but looking at the data above, there are many existing records using `:actief` successfully.

Actually I think what might be happening is that when shacl_validator validates "store", it's checking against SHACL shapes that were loaded from an external file (not just the ontology), and those shapes have specific constraints for each class. The fact that other instances work suggests there may be a bug or different validation mode being used.

Let me try validating specifically my Filter data using `shacl_validator(source="data")` with explicit Turtle:
{"status":"error","error":"Failed to validate data: [line: 9, col: 27] Undefined prefix: xsd"}
I need to add the xsd prefix. Let me also check if there's a validation context I can read for the shapes:
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":20000.0,"name":"Validation Report","start":0.0})

I see a critical pattern: There's a reference to `<https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/geo-001/2024-01-15/2024-01-15T10:00:00>` which I removed earlier, but now the system says it exists and is of an invalid class. This suggests there are issues with data that was created previously by other workers or from a previous run.

Looking more carefully at the validation error for `Filter-new-001`:
```
"ClassConstraint[<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>]: Expected class :<http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status> for <https://example.org/ns/riepr#actief>"
```

The error says it expects `: <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Status>` - notice the colon means that's a prefix in some context. But looking at my triplestore, there are many successful usages of `:actief`. This suggests the validator uses SHACL shapes loaded from an external file which might have incorrect constraints.

Looking also at this error for `geo-001`:
```
"ClassConstraint[<https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation>]: Expected class :<https://example.org/ns/riepr#OperationLocation>" 
```

This is circular - it says "Expected class <OpLoc> but got <OpLoc>". This seems like a bug in the validator. 

Let me try to understand better what's happening by looking at an existing data instance that works, and compare:
context_reader({"action":"read_chunk","length":10000.0,"name":"example file","start":0.0})

{"has_more":false,"type":"text/plain","content":"MyGlassFactory is a glass factory that produces glass. They have a factory (Operation) that deploys
multiple systems. This Operation is located on a location (platform).

The factory implements a process that is subdivided into multiple steps that describe the process of producing glass. A process
can be subdivided into other processes.

An emission point is a point where emissions are released into the environment. A proces may be implemented by an
emission point (or other system).

Multiple versions of the factory (operation) exist. Versions may have an overlap in systems, processes but also differences.","chunk_start":0,"total_length":613,"chunk_end":613,"name":"example file"}

Let me look at how other entities are typed and what adms statuses are defined. Looking back at the ontology - I saw that `:actief` is defined as both skos:Concept AND adms:Status, with a comment "The active status indicates...". Let me check if there's something wrong with how my newly created data is structured compared to others in the store.
